methodsAttempted.query: site:cvut.cz Czech Technical University Prague generative AI ChatGPT policy students teaching
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced the official CTU PDF for framework AI rules and an official faculty research news item that was rejected for claim extraction.

methodsAttempted.query: site:cvut.cz "Framework rules for the use of artificial intelligence" "CTU" "study"
methodsAttempted.notes: Follow-up official-domain search identified the current revised 2024 AI framework guideline, the central legislation page, an older superseded version, and the related graduation-thesis ethics guideline.
robots.txt was checked for www.cvut.cz. The selected public legislation page and PDF paths were not disallowed for User-agent: *; direct HTTP fetches used a descriptive OpenClaw user agent.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a central CTU legislation page and two current CTU methodological guidelines: the revised framework rules for AI use in bachelor and follow-up master study and teaching, and the related graduation-thesis ethics guideline. A superseded 2023 PDF and a faculty research news item were recorded but rejected for claim extraction.

stopConditions: Stop after the current central CTU AI and thesis-ethics guidelines are fetched and citation-ready evidence is extracted.
Do not use older guideline versions or faculty research news to support current CTU-wide claims.
Do not stage raw PDFs, raw HTML, screenshots, browser profiles, or logs in the run directory.

artifacts.claimText: Czech Technical University in Prague has a current central methodological guideline for framework rules on generative AI use by bachelor and follow-up master students and teachers.

citation.citationTitle: Legislation and Study Regulations - Public web - Czech technical university in Prague
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For bachelor and follow-up master study at CTU, AI-tool use in fulfilling programme and subject requirements must be clearly defined and described and must comply with the relevant programme, subject, and thesis-ethics rules.

citation.citationTitle: Framework Rules for the Use of Artificial Intelligence at CTU for Study and Teaching Purposes in Bachelor and Follow-up Master Studies
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: CTU's AI framework identifies sharing sensitive research data, personal data, and contractually concealed data with AI tools as significant cyber risks and states that AI cannot keep confidentiality or protect personal data.

citation.citationTitle: Framework Rules for the Use of Artificial Intelligence at CTU for Study and Teaching Purposes in Bachelor and Follow-up Master Studies

artifacts.claimText: CTU's AI framework marks AI use for examinations, tests, and homework as not appropriate for students unless teacher instructions or the assessment design clearly allow it.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Framework Rules for the Use of Artificial Intelligence at CTU for Study and Teaching Purposes in Bachelor and Follow-up Master Studies
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The use of AI tools in fulfilling the requirements of study programmes and individual subjects must always be clearly defined and described ... and must comply with the rules of the given study programme and the given subjects and the Methodological Guideline on Adherence to Ethical Principles in Preparation of Graduation Theses.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Framework Rules for the Use of Artificial Intelligence at CTU for Study and Teaching Purposes in Bachelor and Follow-up Master Studies
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The use of AI can present significant cyber risks. The most significant risks include the sharing of: sensitive data from ongoing or completed research; personal data ...; data created within the framework of contractual research under an agreement on data concealment. AI is not able to keep confidentiality of shared information or protect personal data.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a source-status claim only; it does not publish a canonical public fact.
Approved because Article 1.3 directly states the requirement; scope is limited to bachelor and follow-up master study.
Approved as a privacy and confidentiality risk claim; no approved-tool or security-review process is inferred.
Approved with wording narrowed to the guideline's examinations, tests, homework, and teacher-instruction context.
Approved as teaching guidance because Article 5.3 uses should, not must.
Approved because the thesis ethics guideline directly names undocumented AI outcomes presented as one's own under plagiarism.

artifacts.title: Czech Technical University in Prague AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Official CTU sources show a current central methodological guideline for framework rules on generative AI use in bachelor and follow-up master study and teaching. The evidence supports narrow claims about programme and subject AI-use rules, privacy and confidentiality risks, restrictions in exams/tests/homework, subject-level teacher guidance, and thesis acknowledgement of AI-tool outcomes. The staged bundle does not claim coverage for doctoral study, staff research policy, procurement, or a university-run approved AI-tool service.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The current AI framework guideline is scoped to bachelor and follow-up master study and teaching; this run does not infer doctoral, research, procurement, or staff-wide AI governance rules.
The official source language for the collected CTU pages and PDFs was English; no Czech-language source text was used for claims in this bundle.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
